input.old_string:
The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
(\texttt{b80f227}) split across two layers.

\paragraph{Layer 1: presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure.} Mathlib's
\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules} module
constructs a monoidal-category structure on the category
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a presheaf of
commutative rings \(R\); in particular, it exposes a tensor-product
operation \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) realising the
sectionwise tensor product
\[
  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
\]
for open sets / objects \(U\) in the indexing site, together with the
associator, left/right unitors, and braiding making the resulting
category symmetric monoidal. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).

\paragraph{Layer 2: scheme-level \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.} The module
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) on a scheme \(X\) (the Lean target
expressed by the namespace \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules})
realises the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
on the small Zariski site of \(X\). It comes with pullback functoriality
(\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\), used heavily in
\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}), pullback comparison isomorphisms
(\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}\),
\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr}\)), and the structure-sheaf
constant module \(\mathcal{O}_X\). It does \emph{not} ship a binary
tensor-product operation
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \to
\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), nor a \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance.

\paragraph{The gap.} The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)
this chapter introduces is the named operation lifting
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) from Layer 1 into
Layer 2, packaged so that the consumer at chapter
\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} can read it as a binary operation on
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\). The data this operation must supply for the group law are the monoidal
\emph{coherence isomorphisms} of \(\otimes_X\) --- the associator
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), the left/right unitors
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) and the braiding
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The unitors and braiding are obtained by the
cheap pattern \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) applied to the corresponding
presheaf-level isomorphism on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\); the associator,
restricted to the \emph{locally trivial} line bundles the group law actually
multiplies, is obtained as the three-step composite of
\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}: apply the sheafification functor to the unit
morphism whiskered by a locally trivial object to absorb each of the two nested
sheafifications (once on each side), with the presheaf-level associator
transported by \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) in between. The only non-formal
input is that the whiskered sheafification unit lies in the localizer class
\(J.W\): local surjectivity is free (right-exactness of \(\otimes\)), and local
injectivity is local-on-target, so it is checked after passing to a trivialising
cover on which the whiskering object is the structure sheaf and the whiskered
unit is carried by the unitor onto the locally injective sheafification unit
itself; the closed bridge \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} then inverts it
under sheafification. This uses no flatness hypothesis. Neither route uses the
abstract \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\) adjoint, a
\(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure on presheaves of modules, or a bundled
\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) instance on the localizer class.
Together with the
\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}
(which carries the inverse existentially) these assemble the commutative monoid
of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units group
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}). The project does \emph{not} require a
global \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance on all quasi-coherent
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), nor a \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure:
monoidal coherence beyond these three isomorphisms is never consumed by a group
law on iso-classes (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}). The
open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is \emph{not} on this critical path and is
retained only as an optional supplement.
The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
independently of any upstream timing.
input.new_string:
The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
(\texttt{b80f227}) assemble into a single \emph{realization route} (route~(e)):
the monoidal structure on the substrate is obtained by instantiating Mathlib's
abstract localization-of-monoidal-categories API, not by hand-assembling the
associator. Four Mathlib facts are the formalization vehicle, all verified
against the on-disk Mathlib source.

\paragraph{(i) The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category.} Mathlib's
\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} constructs a
monoidal-category instance
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\) on
\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \fcomp \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,
\mathtt{RingCat})\), with the sectionwise relative tensor
\[
  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
\]
% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct` /

together with associator, left/right unitors and braiding making it symmetric
monoidal. The base presheaf of rings \(R \fcomp \mathtt{forget}_2\,
\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) is exactly the project's
\(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) carrier (equal by \(\mathtt{rfl}\)),
so this monoidal structure applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
\(\mathcal{O}_X\) --- no project work. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).

\paragraph{(ii) The localization-of-monoidal-categories API.} Mathlib's
\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic} provides the class
\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) and the construction
\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\). The class records exactly
the whiskering-stability data:
% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` and
% `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal`, in
% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean` (L44, L86, L440)
% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean).
% SOURCE QUOTE:
% "class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where

%   whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
%  ...
% def LocalizedMonoidal (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)

%  ...
% noncomputable instance :
%     MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) where ..."
Given \([\MonoidalCategory\,C]\), \([W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}]\), a localization
functor \(L : C \to D\) with \([L.\mathtt{IsLocalization}\,W]\) and an isomorphism
\(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}_C) \cong \mathtt{unit}\), the localized category
\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) is a \(\MonoidalCategory\) with
\emph{all} coherence (associator, unitors, pentagon, triangle, naturalities)
derived. There is \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) anywhere in the
hypothesis chain.

\paragraph{(iii) The proof template for $W$-monoidality.} Mathlib's
\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW} proves
\((J.W\,(A := A)).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) \emph{stalkwise} for presheaves valued in a
\emph{fixed} monoidal concrete category \(A\) (i.e.\ \(C^{op} \to A\)), via the
conservative-family characterisation \(\mathtt{hP.W\_iff}\),
\(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.map\_tensor}\) and \(\mathtt{infer\_instance}\):
% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W`
% and the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance, in
% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (L48, L57)
% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean).
% SOURCE QUOTE:
% "lemma ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W
%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
%     intro Φ
%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
%     infer_instance)"
This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
\(A\).

\paragraph{The gap and route~(e).} The substrate
\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Hence
the monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X =
\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is obtained by applying
\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i) ---
\emph{once} the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is supplied. That instance is
the \emph{sole genuinely-new obligation}: its two whiskering-stability fields are
\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, whose load-bearing local-injectivity half is
\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, the only remaining open sorry. The
proof technique is that of template (iii) ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\):
a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, so
\((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an isomorphism
for \emph{any} \(F\) --- no flatness, no local triviality.

What is \emph{genuinely Mathlib-absent} at the pinned commit: there is no
monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), and no \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)
stalk/fiber/point infrastructure (only \(\mathtt{Presheaf/ColimitFunctor}\)). So
\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) sheafification
localizer must be built project-side, and the two stalk-level ingredients its
proof needs --- (d.1) the module-level stalkwise characterisation of \(J.W\) on
\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor --- are
the residual ported by \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. Once
\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) lands, the associator, unitors and braiding are
\emph{free} from (ii) and the bespoke three-step composite is superseded.

The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
independently of any upstream timing.

\begin{remark}
[The full monoidal category on all modules is off the critical path]
  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This chapter deliberately does
  \emph{not} pursue that, and the relative Picard group law does \emph{not}
  depend on it.

  The reason is that the relative Picard group law of
  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on \emph{isomorphism
  classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
  coherence data of a monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never consumed by the group
  law. Conversely, building the global \(\MonoidalCategory\) on all
  quasi-coherent modules would route through the localization-monoidal
  transport \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal}\), which demands a
  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,W\) instance for the
  sheafification-localizing morphism property \(W\); the
  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) stability \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\)
  of that property, for an \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\), is provable only
  through a \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) ---
  absent from Mathlib at the pinned commit, and a multi-file build. That cost
  buys coherence the group law never uses.

  Crucially, the group law restricts that stability to \emph{locally trivial}
  whiskering objects, where it becomes elementary and needs no closed structure:
  for a locally trivial \(F\) the implication \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft
  g)\), specialised to \(g = \eta\) a sheafification unit, holds because the
  whiskered unit lies in \(J.W\) --- local surjectivity is free
  (right-exactness of \(\otimes\)) and local injectivity is local-on-target,
  checked after passing to a cover on which \(F\) is the structure sheaf and the
  whiskered unit is carried by the unitor onto the locally injective unit itself
  (the argument of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}). This locally-trivial-scoped
  stability is all the group law's associator
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}) consumes, and it sidesteps the
  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) wall that the arbitrary-\(F\) version would hit, as
  well as the sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}
  (which is false over a non-affine open and is therefore off the associator
  critical path).

  The group law needs only the coherence isomorphisms
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}:
  the unitors and braiding by \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) of the
  presheaf-level isomorphisms, and the associator by the locally-trivial-scoped
  three-step composite above. None of these is built from the off-path
  restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. We
  therefore record the global monoidal category as explicitly \emph{off the
  critical path} and build the group law directly on the line-bundle
  subcategory. This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group
  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}),
  whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is transported from
  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\) over
  a fixed ring and needs no sheafification, no localization, and no
  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\).
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}
[The full monoidal category is cheap via route~(e); the group law consumes only propositions]
  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
        lem:whisker_of_W}
  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. Under route~(e) this is
  \emph{cheap}, not expensive: it is exactly the output of
  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), once the single instance
  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}) is supplied. All the
  coherence is then derived by the API.

  Crucially, \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) needs \emph{no} closed structure. Its
  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\) (tensoring with an isomorphism
  preserves isomorphisms). This is the same technique Mathlib uses for
  fixed-base presheaves in
  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\), ported to
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). It uses neither
  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
  false over a non-affine open and is therefore off the critical path); the
  earlier reading that arbitrary-\(F\) whisker stability requires
  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) was the \emph{wrong altitude}.

  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, all now supplied by the route~(e) monoidal structure;
  none of them is built from the off-path restriction-compatibility isomorphism
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group
  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}),
  whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is transported from
  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\): there
  over a fixed ring, here over the sheafification localizer via
  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\).
\end{remark}

Mathlib identity at one morphism; this lemma is the thin wrapper used by the
  absorption steps of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}.
\end{proof}

\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}

The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
whiskering-stability lemmas below. Their load-bearing local-injectivity half is
the sole remaining open obligation of the entire group-law engine.

\begin{lemma}
[Local injectivity of a whiskered localizer morphism (stalkwise, flatness-free)]
  \label{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally
  bijective morphisms). Let \(F\) be an \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of
  \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of
  \(R\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R).\mathrm{map}\,g\) lies in \(J.W\). Then the
  left-whiskered morphism
  \[
    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
  \]
  is \emph{\(J\)-locally injective}. No flatness and no local-triviality
  hypothesis on \(F\) is required.

  This is the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\)-field content (the injectivity half) of the
  instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), and it is
  \emph{the sole remaining open obligation for the whole group-law engine}: every
  other ingredient of route~(e) is either supplied by Mathlib or closed
  project-side.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
  The argument is the stalkwise one used by Mathlib for fixed-base presheaves in
  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\)
  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}), ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). A
  morphism in \(J.W\) is, on the topological site of \(X\), a \emph{stalkwise
  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving
  \[
    (F \triangleleft g)_x
      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes_{R_x} g_x
      \;\colon\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} M_x \;\longrightarrow\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} N_x .
  \]
  Tensoring an isomorphism \(g_x\) by the identity \(\mathrm{id}_{F_x}\) yields an
  isomorphism --- no flatness is needed, since \(g_x\) is invertible, not merely
  injective. Hence \(F \triangleleft g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, in particular
  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).

  The two ingredients this stalkwise computation needs are, at the pinned commit,
  absent from Mathlib at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level, and constitute
  the genuine residual:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item[(d.1)] the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level \(J.W\) on
      \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) (that \(g \in J.W\) is equivalent to \(g_x\) being an
      isomorphism for every \(x\)); and
    \item[(d.2)] the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x \otimes_{R_x}
      B_x\) of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
      module-presheaf tensor (using that \(\mathtt{tensorLeft}\) /
      \(\mathtt{tensorRight}\) preserve filtered colimits over a module category).
  \end{enumerate}
  Mathlib supplies the analogues for presheaves valued in a fixed monoidal
  concrete category, but not for modules over a varying ring; porting (d.1)+(d.2)
  to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) is the remaining work.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
  \label{lem:whisker_of_W}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W, PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
  With notation as in \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: for an
  \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of \(R\)-modules \(F\) and a morphism \(g\) whose
  underlying additive-presheaf map lies in \(J.W\), both whiskerings again lie in
  \(J.W\):
  \[
    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; F \triangleleft g \in J.W,
    \qquad
    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; g \triangleright F \in J.W .
  \]
  These two statements are precisely the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) and
  \(\mathtt{whiskerRight}\) data required by the class
  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) for
  the morphism property \(J.W\); together they furnish the instance
  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) of \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}. No flatness and no
  local triviality is used --- they are the flatness-free replacements for the
  flat variants of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
  By the locally-bijective characterisation of \(J.W\), it suffices to show
  \(F \triangleleft g\) is both locally injective and locally surjective.
  \emph{Local surjectivity} is free and needs no hypothesis on \(F\): the presheaf
  tensor is right exact, so \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the locally-zero cokernel
  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\), which is therefore locally
  zero (Mathlib \(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)). \emph{Local
  injectivity} is \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. The two together
  give \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
  braiding of the symmetric presheaf monoidal structure, which is an isomorphism
  and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[The \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal substrate (route~(e) target)]
  \label{lem:jw_ismonoidal}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:whisker_of_W}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The sheafification localizer \(J.W\) on
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) is \emph{monoidal}: it carries an
  instance
  \[
    J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal},
  \]
  whose two fields are the whiskering-stability lemmas \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}
  (and which is multiplicative because sheafification inverts identities and
  composites). Consequently, by Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)
  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) applied to the already-monoidal category
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\), the localization functor
  \(L = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) (which is the localization at
  \(J.W\)), and the unit isomorphism \(L(\mathbf{1}) \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), the
  category
  \[
    \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
      \;=\; \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
  \]
  acquires a full \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure whose tensor is the substrate
  \(\otimes_X\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) and whose associator,
  left/right unitors, braiding and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
  naturalities) are derived by the API. This is the route~(e) target: the
  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} are
  then components of this derived monoidal structure rather than hand-assembled
  composites.

  This block states the route~(e) construction; it is currently
  \emph{stated-but-unformalized} (no Lean declaration realizes the
  \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance yet), pending the sole open obligation
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. It mirrors Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) construction one categorical level up.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:whisker_of_W}
  Multiplicativity of \(J.W\) (closure under identities and composition) is part
  of its being a localizer (\(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). The two
  whiskering-stability fields of \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) are
  exactly the left- and right-whiskering statements of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.
  Hence \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) holds. Sheafification of presheaves of
  modules is a localization at \(J.W\) (the underlying identification of
  sheafification with localization at the locally-bijective class), and the unit
  presheaf sheafifies to \(\mathcal{O}_X\); feeding these to
  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) produces the \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with all coherence derived, as in
  \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}. No \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure enters
  at any point.
\end{proof}

  \(\eta_A = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) is locally injective
  (\(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_toSheafify}\)), hence so is its restriction to the
  cover; gluing the per-\(V\) injectivity along the cover, \(\eta_A \triangleright
  F^{\flat}\) is locally injective. (The sieve bookkeeping is exactly that of the
  flat-whiskering injectivity argument
  \(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_whiskerLeft\_of\_flat}\); its
  \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_exact}\) injectivity step is the only thing
  swapped --- replaced here by the trivialisation
  \(F^{\flat}(V) \cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) on the cover and the right unitor.) The
  left-whiskered statement \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_A\) is identical with the
  left unitor \(\lambda\) in place of \(\rho\).

  Local injectivity together with local surjectivity gives
  \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat} \in J.W\) (resp.\ \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft
  \eta_A \in J.W\)). Applying the closed bridge
  \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}, the sheafification \(a\) sends each such
  whiskered unit to an isomorphism.

  \emph{The nested form.} Unfolding the substrate on both sides exposes an inner
  sheafification:
  \[
    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr),
    \qquad
    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr).
  \]
  The required isomorphism is the three-step composite:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \emph{Absorb the inner sheafification on the left.} The unit
      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
      right-whiskering by the locally trivial object \(P\),
      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}} \triangleright P^{\flat}\), again
      lies in \(J.W\) by the key claim above (\(P\) locally trivial). By
      \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} the sheafification functor \(a\)
      inverts it:
      \[
        a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr).
      \]
    \item \emph{Transport the presheaf associator.} Apply \(a\) to \(\alpha\):
      \[
        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr)
          \;\xrightarrow[\ \sim\ ]{\;a.\mathtt{mapIso}\,\alpha\;}\;
        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr).
      \]
    \item \emph{Restore the inner sheafification on the right.} The unit
      \(\eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
      left-whiskering by the locally trivial object \(M\),
      \(M^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\), again
      lies in \(J.W\) by the key claim above (\(M\) locally trivial). By
      \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} \(a\) inverts it:
      \[
        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr)
          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
        a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
          \;=\; M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P).
      \]
  \end{enumerate}
  Composing the three isomorphisms yields
  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim} M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\).
  Only steps 1 and 3 carry non-formal content, namely the key claim that the
  whiskered unit lies in \(J.W\); step 2 is the image under \(a\) of the presheaf
  monoidal associator already supplied by Mathlib.

  This is an objectwise isomorphism for the three fixed locally trivial objects,
  not a natural associator on a category: no pentagon identity is constructed or
  used. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that is
  required. The construction works \emph{entirely at the presheaf/section level on
  a trivialising cover}: each absorption isomorphism comes from checking the
  local-on-target injectivity of a whiskered sheafification unit after restricting
  to a cover on which the whiskering object is the structure sheaf, then inverting
  under \(a\) via \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}. It uses
  \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) and
  \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}): because the presheaf tensor is sectionwise,
  restriction to the cover is plain evaluation at smaller opens, and no
  sheafified-tensor pullback-compatibility iso is needed.
\end{proof}

\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:jw_ismonoidal}
  % NOTE: under route~(e) the associator is the API-derived associator of the
  % LocalizedMonoidal monoidal structure (lem:jw_ismonoidal); it is NOT a
  % hand-assembled three-step composite. The `IsLocallyTrivial` hypotheses on
  % M,N,P are vestigial — they are received to match the current Lean pin but are
  % NOT proof ingredients (the API-derived associator is natural in arbitrary
  % objects) and may be dropped once lem:jw_ismonoidal lands.
  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
  \(M, N, P \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). There is an isomorphism
  \[
    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
  \]
  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). This is the associator component of the
  monoidal structure of \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}, read objectwise; as such it is
  natural in \(M, N, P\). The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
  isomorphism, the proposition \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X
  P \cong M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\).

  The current Lean pin additionally carries \emph{locally trivial}
  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\), \cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial})
  hypotheses on \(M, N, P\); under route~(e) these are \emph{vestigial} --- they
  are not ingredients of the API-derived associator and may be dropped once
  \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} is in place.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:jw_ismonoidal}
  By \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}, the substrate \(\otimes_X\) is the tensor of a
  \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) obtained
  from Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)
  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}). The required isomorphism is the associator
  \(\alpha_{M,N,P}\) of that monoidal structure, which the API derives together
  with the pentagon and all naturality data; no hand-assembled composite is
  needed. Reading \(\alpha_{M,N,P}\) at the three fixed objects gives the
  objectwise isomorphism, and discarding the witness gives the proposition
  \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\) that the group law consumes. The
  construction uses \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}
  \,R)\) and \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}); the sole non-formal input upstream is the
  whisker-stability of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, whose open half is
  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. The locally-trivial hypotheses of
  the statement are not consumed.
\end{proof}

[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the \(\otimes\)-invertible carrier
  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  % NOTE (iter-213): prose↔Lean predicate mismatch. The Lean `tensorObjOnProduct`
  % takes `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT`, which is the IsLocallyTrivial subtype
  % (defined in LineBundlePullback.lean), and its body uses only
  % `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` directly — NOT `IsInvertible`,
  % `tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid`, or the coherence isos as the prose/\uses below
  % claim. Blueprint-writer should reconcile the carrier predicate (IsLocallyTrivial
  % vs IsInvertible) and the \uses. See task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-ts213.md.
  % NOTE: the coherence isos
  % \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
  % and the units-of-monoid engine \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} supply
  % the group-law data. \texttt{IsLocallyTrivial} is a separate predicate,
  % connected to \texttt{IsInvertible} only by an off-critical-path equivalence.
  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
  \(\otimes\)-invertible subtype
  \[
    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
    \;\;=\;\;
    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M \,\bigr\}
  \]
  of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}. The bifunctor
  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
  if \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M'\) then
  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\); the unit
  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible; and every invertible module
  carries its own tensor inverse. Hence the isomorphism classes of the subtype
  form the units group of \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} on
  \(C \times_S T\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  Closure under \(\otimes\) is the units-closed-under-multiplication statement of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}: if \(M \otimes N \cong \mathcal{O}\)
  and \(M' \otimes N' \cong \mathcal{O}\), then
  \((M \otimes M') \otimes (N \otimes N') \cong \mathcal{O}\) by the coherence
  isomorphisms, so \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes M')\) with witness
  \(N \otimes N'\). The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible with
  witness itself (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the tensor inverse is
  carried by the predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Thus the
  subtype is a submonoid all of whose elements are units --- the abelian group
  of iso-classes that is the units group of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} restricted to \(C \times_S T\).

[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial carrier
  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,
        lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
  \emph{locally-trivial} subtype
  \[
    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
    \;\;=\;\;
    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M \,\bigr\}
  \]
  introduced in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. The bifunctor
  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
  if \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M'\) then
  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\). Hence \(\otimes\)
  closes on \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\), supplying the binary
  operation the relative group law multiplies with.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
  Closure is exactly
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}: the tensor product of two
  locally-trivial rank-one modules is again locally trivial of rank one
  (\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_isLocallyTrivial}\)). The Lean construction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjOnProduct}\) applies this directly to a pair of objects of
  the \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) subtype \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\),
  returning the tensor with its locally-trivial witness; no
  \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate and no coherence isomorphism enters this
  closure statement. (The \(\otimes\)-invertibility carrier
  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible} is a
  separate predicate, connected to \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) only by an
  off-critical-path equivalence; the units-group packaging is
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, not this lemma.)

Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
  equivalently, an abelian group. Multiplication is
  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\), well-defined on isomorphism classes
  because \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically)
  and the tensor product of two \(\otimes\)-invertibles is again
  \(\otimes\)-invertible (closure); the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and
  associativity, unitality and commutativity are the coherence isomorphisms
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (on the invertible objects in play),
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as
  equalities of iso-classes. The inverse of \([M]\) is the class of the tensor
  inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) carried existentially
  by \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), so
  every element is a unit. This abelian group is the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\)
  (Stacks tag 01CR). It is, equivalently, the group of units of the commutative
  monoid of \emph{all} iso-classes; that global monoid is not built here, since
  the associator on arbitrary modules is not constructed and the group law
  consumes only the objectwise coherence isomorphisms on invertible objects.
  This mirrors Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\)
  and the transported \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), used here as the design
  template; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
  the pinned commit.

  The associativity datum this group law consumes is the locally-trivial-scoped
  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}. If the \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
  carrier form is retained for the group law, the only added obligation is the
  bridge \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) --- the
  standard fact that an invertible sheaf is locally free of rank one --- which
  supplies the local-triviality hypotheses of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; this
  bridge is itself off the associator critical path.
\end{lemma}
input.new_string:
Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
  equivalently, an abelian group --- the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag
  01CR).

  Route~(e) supplies this directly. By \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} the category
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
  is a (symmetric) \(\MonoidalCategory\) with tensor \(\otimes_X\) and unit
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), obtained from \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\).
  Its set of isomorphism classes --- the skeleton \(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\Scheme.%
  \mathtt{Modules}\,X)\) --- is therefore a commutative monoid under
  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\): the multiplication is well-defined because
  \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality});
  the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and associativity, unitality and commutativity
  are the existence of the coherence isomorphisms
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
  read as equalities of iso-classes. The Picard group is then the group of
  \emph{units} of this commutative monoid,
  \[
    \Pic(X)
      \;\;=\;\;
    \mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X)\bigr),
  \]
  exactly as Mathlib builds
  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}
  \,R))\) and transports \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\). A class \([M]\) is a unit
  precisely when \(M\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse being carried
  existentially by \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\). The pinned carrier
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid}\) is this commutative monoid of all
  iso-classes; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
  the pinned commit.

  Because the route~(e) associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} is natural in
  \emph{arbitrary} objects (it is the associator of the
  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) structure, not a locally-trivial-scoped
  composite), the group law needs \emph{no} auxiliary bridge from
  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) to \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\): the coherence
  isomorphisms apply to the invertible objects directly.
\end{lemma}

  Restrict attention to \(\otimes\)-invertible objects. Closure holds: if
  \(M, N\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible with inverses \(M', N'\), then
  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X (M' \otimes_X N') \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) by the
  coherence isomorphisms, so \(M \otimes_X N\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible.
  Well-definedness of \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\) on iso-classes is the
  bifunctoriality of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}. The
  group axioms are propositions about iso-classes and are discharged by the
  coherence isomorphisms, each evaluated on the invertible objects in play:
  associativity by \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (whose hypotheses are exactly
  \(\otimes\)-invertibility of the three arguments), the unit laws by
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, and commutativity by
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}; no pentagon, triangle or hexagon identity is
  invoked, since each axiom asserts only the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism.
  Every class \([M]\) has an inverse \([N]\) with
  \([M \otimes_X N] = [\mathcal{O}_X]\), namely the tensor inverse carried by
  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}).
  Therefore the iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible modules form an abelian
  group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR) --- equivalently the
  group of units of the (un-built) commutative monoid of all iso-classes. The
  whole assembly follows the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\)
  template.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality, lem:jw_ismonoidal,
        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
  By \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} the substrate \(\otimes_X\) is the tensor of a
  monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with unit
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\). Its skeleton --- the set of isomorphism classes --- is a
  commutative monoid under \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\): multiplication is
  well-defined by the bifunctoriality of
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, and the monoid axioms are
  the existence of the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and (commutativity) the braiding
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as equalities of iso-classes; no pentagon,
  triangle or hexagon identity is invoked, since each axiom asserts only the
  \emph{existence} of an isomorphism. Passing to units: a class \([M]\) is
  invertible in this monoid precisely when \(M\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible, with
  inverse \([N]\) for the witness \(N\) of \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Hence
  \(\mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X))\) is an abelian
  group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR) --- assembled exactly by
  the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\) template used for
  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\).
\end{proof}

734	\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
735	\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}
736	
737	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
738	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
739	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
740	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
741	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
742	sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
743	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
744	whiskering-stability lemmas below. Their load-bearing local-injectivity half is
745	the sole remaining open obligation of the entire group-law engine.
746	
747	\begin{lemma}
748	[Local injectivity of a whiskered localizer morphism (stalkwise, flatness-free)]
749	  \label{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
750	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W}
751	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
752	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
753	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally
754	  bijective morphisms). Let \(F\) be an \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of
755	  \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of
756	  \(R\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
757	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R).\mathrm{map}\,g\) lies in \(J.W\). Then the
758	  left-whiskered morphism
759	  \[
760	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
761	  \]
762	  is \emph{\(J\)-locally injective}. No flatness and no local-triviality
763	  hypothesis on \(F\) is required.
764	
765	  This is the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\)-field content (the injectivity half) of the
766	  instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), and it is
767	  \emph{the sole remaining open obligation for the whole group-law engine}: every
768	  other ingredient of route~(e) is either supplied by Mathlib or closed
769	  project-side.
770	\end{lemma}
771	
772	\begin{proof}
773	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
774	  The argument is the stalkwise one used by Mathlib for fixed-base presheaves in
775	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\)
776	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}), ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). A
777	  morphism in \(J.W\) is, on the topological site of \(X\), a \emph{stalkwise
778	  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
779	  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
780	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving
781	  \[
782	    (F \triangleleft g)_x
783	      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes_{R_x} g_x
784	      \;\colon\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} M_x \;\longrightarrow\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} N_x .
785	  \]
786	  Tensoring an isomorphism \(g_x\) by the identity \(\mathrm{id}_{F_x}\) yields an
787	  isomorphism --- no flatness is needed, since \(g_x\) is invertible, not merely
788	  injective. Hence \(F \triangleleft g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, in particular
789	  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).
790	
791	  The two ingredients this stalkwise computation needs are, at the pinned commit,
792	  absent from Mathlib at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level, and constitute
793	  the genuine residual:
794	  \begin{enumerate}
795	    \item[(d.1)] the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level \(J.W\) on
796	      \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) (that \(g \in J.W\) is equivalent to \(g_x\) being an
797	      isomorphism for every \(x\)); and
798	    \item[(d.2)] the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x \otimes_{R_x}
799	      B_x\) of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
800	      module-presheaf tensor (using that \(\mathtt{tensorLeft}\) /
801	      \(\mathtt{tensorRight}\) preserve filtered colimits over a module category).
802	  \end{enumerate}
803	  Mathlib supplies the analogues for presheaves valued in a fixed monoidal
804	  concrete category, but not for modules over a varying ring; porting (d.1)+(d.2)
805	  to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) is the remaining work.
806	\end{proof}
807	
808	\begin{lemma}
809	[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]

# Blueprint Writer Report

## Slug
ts-routee214b

## Status
COMPLETE

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex

## Summary
Pivoted the associator/group-law realization from the hand-assembled
absorb→associate→absorb 3-step composite to **route (e)** — instantiating
Mathlib's `Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal` on the already-monoidal
`PresheafOfModules O_X` and the sheafification localizer `J.W`, so the
associator/unitors/braiding/all coherence are API-derived. Cleared all four
iter-213 lean-vs-blueprint must-fix items. No `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` markers were
added or removed.

## Changes Made
- **Revised motivation paragraph** (`sec:tensorobj_motivation`) — replaced the
  prose that built requirement (3) as the bespoke 3-step composite with the
  route-(e) story: the full monoidal scaffolding is already in Mathlib; the sole
  new obligation is the instance `J.W.IsMonoidal`, whose load-bearing half is the
  flatness-free stalkwise local-injectivity lemma (now named the sole open
  obligation).
- **Rewrote the API survey** (`sec:tensorobj_api_survey`) — replaced the
  Layer-1/Layer-2/gap framing with the four verified route-(e) Mathlib facts:
  (i) `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, (ii)
  `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` + `Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal`,
  (iii) `Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW` as the proof template, and the
  Mathlib-absent gap (no monoidal `SheafOfModules`, no `PresheafOfModules`
  stalk infra). Added verbatim `% SOURCE` / `% SOURCE QUOTE` comments quoting the
  on-disk Mathlib source for (i),(ii),(iii).
- **Reframed `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`** — the full monoidal
  category is now documented as *cheap* (the output of `LocalizedMonoidal`, no
  `MonoidalClosed`), the earlier "needs MonoidalClosed" reading explicitly called
  the wrong altitude; the group law still consumes only existence-of-iso
  propositions. `\uses` now includes `lem:whisker_of_W`.
- **Updated `lem:flat_whisker_localizer` prose** — flat whisker variants kept as
  closed standalone results, documented as **superseded on the critical path** by
  the flatness-free `_of_W` variants (`lem:whisker_of_W`).
- **ADDED `\section{Route~(e)...}`** (`sec:tensorobj_route_e`) with three new
  blocks:
  - `lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W` —
    `\lean{PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W}`. Documents the
    load-bearing residual (a `sorry`): stalkwise `(F◁g)_x = id⊗g_x` iso, with d.1
    (module-level `J.W`↔stalk-iso) and d.2 (stalk commutes with relative tensor)
    named as the Mathlib-absent residual. Marked THE sole open obligation.
  - `lem:whisker_of_W` —
    `\lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W, PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W}`.
    The closed flatness-free whisker lemmas (surjectivity free, injectivity via
    the residual); the `whiskerLeft`/`whiskerRight` data of `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal (J.W)`.
  - `lem:jw_ismonoidal` — **unpinned** (no Lean decl yet), stated-but-unformalized:
    the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance + `LocalizedMonoidal` instantiation as the
    route-(e) target giving `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)`.
- **Rewrote `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`** — kept the `\lean{}` pin; statement/proof
  now obtain the associator as the API-derived component of `lem:jw_ismonoidal`,
  NOT a 3-step composite. `IsLocallyTrivial` hypotheses documented as vestigial.
  `\uses` → `{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:jw_ismonoidal}`. Removed the dead
  flatness/cover-route prose.
- **Fixed `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`** (must-fix) — carrier corrected to the
  `IsLocallyTrivial` subtype `LineBundle.OnProduct` (not `IsInvertible`); proof
  now uses `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` directly, matching the Lean body. `\uses`
  dropped `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible` and `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`,
  now `{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}`.
- **Reframed `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`** — kept the
  `tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` carrier pinned; the commutative monoid + its
  units now follow from the route-(e) `MonoidalCategory` via
  `Pic(X) = Units(Skeleton(Scheme.Modules X))`, mirroring `CommRing.Pic`. Removed
  the `IsInvertible⇒IsLocallyTrivial` bridge clause (no longer needed: the
  route-(e) associator is natural in arbitrary objects). `\uses` gained
  `lem:jw_ismonoidal`.
- **Updated minor cross-references** — the `lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W`
  bridge re-documented as a retained closed supplement (no longer the
  associator's load-bearing step); the `tensorObj_unit_iso` proof's "as with the
  associator" phrasing fixed; the `sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift` intro and the
  `sec:tensorobj_consistency_check` itemization rewritten to the route-(e) chain.

## Cross-references introduced
- `\uses{lem:jw_ismonoidal}` added in `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
  `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`, `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`
  (via `lem:whisker_of_W`) — all three new labels are defined in this chapter
  (`sec:tensorobj_route_e`).
- `\uses{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}` in `lem:whisker_of_W`; defined in
  this chapter.
- `\uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}` in
  `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct` — defined earlier in this chapter.
- LaTeX environment balance verified (non-comment): lemma 16/16, proof 17/17,
  definition 2/2, theorem 1/1, remark 1/1, enumerate 2/2.

## References consulted
- `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean`
  (L104, L125) — verbatim `monoidalCategoryStruct`/`monoidalCategory` instance for
  the `% SOURCE QUOTE` in survey paragraph (i).
- `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean`
  (L44, L86, L440) — verbatim `IsMonoidal` class, `LocalizedMonoidal` def, and the
  `MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal …)` instance for survey paragraph (ii).
- `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean`
  (L48, L57) — verbatim `isMonoidal_W` lemma + instance for survey paragraph (iii).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (L382–478 `WhiskerOfW`
  section) — confirmed the three pinned Lean decl names and that `WhiskerOfW` is a
  `section` (so names are `PresheafOfModules.*`, not `*.WhiskerOfW.*`).
- `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-ts213.md` — the four must-fix
  items addressed.

Note on citation discipline: the new `% SOURCE`/`% SOURCE QUOTE` blocks cite the
on-disk Mathlib `.lean` source (the route-(e) formalization vehicle), as the
directive explicitly authorized; the parenthetical points to the exact
`.lake/packages/mathlib/...` file read this session. The pre-existing
Stacks/Kleiman citation blocks (`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`,
`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`,
`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`) were left untouched.

## Macros needed (if any)
None. (I initially used a nonexistent `\fcomp`; replaced both occurrences with
`\circ`. All other commands — `\MonoidalCategory`, `\Scheme`, `\Pic`,
`\AddCommGroup`, `\triangleleft`, `\triangleright` — are pre-existing.)

## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
None — no new external source was needed (route (e) is a Mathlib-API construction;
the underlying "Pic = Units of the monoidal Skeleton" math is standard and already
cited from Stacks 01CR / Kleiman in the retained blocks).

## Notes for Plan Agent
- `lem:jw_ismonoidal` is intentionally **unpinned** (stated-but-unformalized):
  there is no Lean declaration realizing the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance or the
  `LocalizedMonoidal` `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)` yet. If a
  prover/scaffolder produces those decls, `lem:jw_ismonoidal` should get a
  `\lean{}` pin (e.g. the `J.W.IsMonoidal` instance + the localized monoidal
  instance), and the vestigial `IsLocallyTrivial` hypotheses on
  `tensorObj_assoc_iso` can then be dropped from the Lean signature.
- The chapter now declares `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (a `sorry`) as
  THE single remaining open obligation for the whole group-law engine, with the
  two Mathlib-absent ingredients d.1/d.2 named — this is the natural next prover
  target, requiring a `PresheafOfModules`-level stalk/point port that Mathlib does
  not yet have.

## Strategy-modifying findings
None. The pivot is an API-realization change at the altitude the strategy already
anticipated (route (e)); the substrate definition `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`
is unchanged and the consumer chain is unaffected.
